it:96.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:96.3:
"Tied by the yoke of sensuality and the yoke of becoming, one is a returner, returning to this state. Untied from the yoke of sensuality but tied by the yoke of becoming, one is a non-returner, not returning to this state. Untied from
the yoke of sensuality and from the yoke of becoming, one is an arahant whose fermentations are ended."
it:96.4: Tied by both the yoke of sensuality and the yoke of becoming, beings go to the wandering-on leading to birth and death.
it:96.5: Those who've abandoned the sensual without reaching the ending of fermentations, are tied by the yoke of becoming, are said to be non-returners.
it:96.6: While those who've cut off doubt have no more conceit or further becoming. They who have reached the ending of fermentations, while in the world have gone beyond.
it:96.7: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
